canonical_nt.summary: Named NT defect tranche is complete and guarded.

canonical_ot.summary: Canonical OT non-Psalms passed final trust readiness after ordinary OT completeness, recovery consolidation, trust-boundary checks, Nehemiah 7:73 adapter policy, and Daniel 11 source-absent governed exclusion. This does not trust Psalms or the whole corpus.

psalms.summary: Psalms are shape-governed as a 155-record legacy psalter subsystem, but trust-readiness classification failed because provenance and lane identity remain unadjudicated.

deuterocanon.summary: Deuterocanon final trust-readiness classification is recorded as not trust-ready. Greek Esther and Greek Daniel require manual form and versification adjudication before repair. 4 Maccabees 10:4 and 11:7-8 remain governed variant-footnote material, not active primary rows. No Bible text mutation was performed.

broader_canon.summary: Broader canon final trust-readiness classification is recorded as not trust-ready. The raw corpus is governed, but the lane still contains raw-text-only and zero-row records and has not received source-family, translation-lane, or textual-collation adjudication. No Bible text mutation was performed.

vulgate.summary: Vulgate pilot lanes are committed as bounded source-lane data. Vulgate build, expansion, source acquisition, and integration are deferred until the active non-Vulgate corpus is trusted; existing Vulgate pilot work may be audited later.

blockers: Psalm text provenance is not adjudicated.
NRSV Psalms key is not promoted as a trusted Bible NRSV lane.
Grail1963 is a liturgical psalter lane with separate conventions.
Psalms 151-155 are Orthodox appendix material, not normal canonical OT chapter rows.
latestPsalmsTrustReadinessClassification.summary: Psalms are shape-governed and internally counted, but not trust-ready. Record count and translation-count checks passed, but provenance and lane identity must be adjudicated before any Psalms textual trust claim.

latestCanonicalOTFinalTrustAudit.summary: Canonical OT non-Psalms final trust readiness passed after ordinary OT completeness, recovery consolidation, trust boundary, Daniel 11 governed source-absent exclusion, and Nehemiah 7:73 adapter policy checks. This is not a whole-Bible or whole-OT-with-Psalms trust claim.

latestCanonicalOTDaniel11Policy.summary: The preserved controlled NABRE source lane at commit d0eed049 contains Daniel chapter 11 with an empty verses array. Daniel 11:1-45 remains without NABRE active text by governed exclusion. Do not synthesize NABRE, scrape substitute text, or overlay from another translation. Canonical OT may proceed only to final trust audit with this exclusion recorded.

vulgateDeferralPolicy.summary: Do not build, expand, source-acquire, or integrate Vulgate until the active non-Vulgate corpus is trusted. After active non-Vulgate trust is established, audit the existing Vulgate pilot state before deciding whether any further Vulgate buildout is authorized.

latestCanonicalOTNehemiah773AdapterPolic.summary: Nehemiah 7:73 is not a missing-row defect. NRSV/Rotherham/KJV active row 7:73 contains both the settlement close and the seventh-month transition. NABRE places the settlement close at 7:72 and the seventh-month transition at 8:1. Preserve source text by source address; any harmonized display requires adapter/view policy rather than synthetic row construction.

latestGreekEstherFormPolicy.summary: Active Greek Esther is governed as NRSV named-Addition form plus ordinary Esther chapters. DRB/NABRE row-grid deltas are not repair instructions. Do not rewrite to DRB numbered extension chapters, NABRE row grid, or ordinary Esther-only form without a separate canonical-form migration policy.

latestDeuterocanonSourceShapeReview.summary: Greek Esther and Greek Daniel source-shape review found no safe simple overlay. Greek Esther has substantial active/DRB/ordinary Esther form divergence. Greek Daniel is closer to DRB but remaining mismatches fall in Greek-addition territory. No Bible text was changed.

latestMaccabees34SourceState.summary: Current active 3 and 4 Maccabees files already carry NRSV source-backed repair metadata from BibleGateway NRSVA and the historical repair commit 8177b52. Raw source acquisition is no longer the next blocker for these files; further work is bounded source/form review.

latestMaccabees4VariantFootnotePolicy.summary: BibleGateway NRSVA presents 4 Maccabees 10:4 and 11:7-8 as other-authority footnote additions, not primary body-text rows. They remain governed variant material and must not be inserted into active rows without a separate variant apparatus policy.

latestGreekDanielFormPolicy.summary: Active Greek Daniel is governed as one NRSV extended Daniel book: Daniel 1-12, Prayer of Azariah/Song embedded in chapter 3, Susanna as chapter 13, and Bel and the Dragon as chapter 14. DRB/NABRE row-grid deltas are not repair instructions.

blockers: Deuterocanon governance explicitly remains not trusted/underbuilt after prior tranches.
Greek Esther requires manual Catholic/Greek Esther form and versification review before any repair.
Greek Daniel requires manual form and versification policy before any repair.
4 Maccabees 10:4 and 11:7-8 are governed variant-footnote material, not active primary rows.
Greek Esther simple overlay remains blocked.
Greek Daniel simple overlay remains blocked.
4 Maccabees variant-footnote insertion remains blocked without a variant apparatus policy.
Broader canon raw corpus is governed but remains not trusted.
Broader canon governance audit found 38 candidate records.
Broader canon governance audit found 36 raw-text-only records.
Broader canon governance audit found 2 zero-row records.
Broader canon material has not received source-family and translation-lane adjudication.
Broader canon material has not received final textual collation sufficient for a trust claim.

latestActiveNonVulgateTrustBoundaryRevie.summary: Active non-Vulgate Bible corpus lanes are now explicitly classified, but the active non-Vulgate corpus is not globally trusted. Canonical NT named-defect tranche is complete and canonical OT non-Psalms is trusted with governed exclusions. Psalms, deuterocanon, and broader canon remain not trust-ready under recorded blockers. Vulgate remains deferred and audit-only.
